LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS JUSTIN CHEUNG, KELVIN CHEUNG AND VILMA CHEUNG'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT, AS TO ALL EIGHT CAUSES OF ACTION IN THE COMPLAINT FILED BY PLAINTIFF CHAO TAN IS SUSTAINED AS TO ALL EIGHT CAUSES OF ACTION AND THE CASE IS STAYED PENDING MS. TAN'S FILING A PETITION IN THE PROBATE DIVISION AND, IF SHE DOES SO, SHE MAY REQUEST THAT THE PROBATE DIVISION CONSOLIDATE THIS CASE WITH THE PROBATE CASE. THE STAY SHALL BE FOR SIX MONTHS FROM THIS DATE AND SUBJECT TO DISMISSAL OF THE ACTION IF NO PROBATE ACTION IS FILED BY THE END OF THE SIX MONTH PERIOD. ALL EIGHT CAUSES OF ACTION ARE BASED ON THE ALLEGATION THAT, AS A RESULT OF THE DEATH OF HER HUSBAND, MONIES UNDER THE CONTROL OF DEFENDANTS NOW BELONG TO MS. TAN. THIS ALLEGATION CONCERNS THE ADMINISTRATION OF A DECEDENT'S ESTATE AND THUS ALL EIGHT CAUSES ARE WITHIN THE EXCLUSIVE JURISDICTION OF THAT DIVISION. (SEE RUTTER GROUP PROBATE PRACTICE GUIDE 3:59 ("ONLY THE PROBATE DEPARTMENTS OF THE SUPERIOR COURT ARE EMPOWERED TO ACT IN A DECEDENT'S ESTATE ADMINISTRATION" (EMPHASIS IN ORIGINAL)). WRITTEN ORDER IS SIGNED AND FILED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN: J. RIOS MERIDA; REPORTER: LAURA REDING, CSR NO. 9711, E-MAIL: LREDING@SFTC.ORG

LAW AND MOTION, PLAINTIFF CHAO HONG TAN'S MOTION FOR ORDER CONSOLIDATING CASES. (CASE(S) PES-18-302013 WITH CASE CGC-17-560310). PLAINTIFF CHAO HONG TAN'S MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE THIS CASE WITH CASE PES-18-30213 IS GRANTED. THE TWO CASES ARE CONSOLIDATED FOR ALL PURPOSES. THE LEAD CASE IS PES-18-30213 AND ALL FURTHER FILINGS WILL BE IN THAT CASE ONLY. ALL FUTURE DATES SET IN THIS CASE ARE VACATED. CONSOLIDATING THE TWO CASES IS IN THE INTERESTS OF JUDICIAL ECONOMY AND WILL AVOID POSSIBLE INCONSISTENT RESULTS. MAKING THE PROBATE CASE THE LEAD CASE IS IN RECOGNITION THAT BOTH CASES RAISE ISSUES THAT ARE PROPERLY HANDLED IN THE PROBATE DIVISION OF THIS COURT, NOT THE CIVIL DIVISION. WHILE MS. TAN COULD HAVE ACTED WITH GREATER DILIGENCE, HER FAILURE TO DO SO IS NOT A BASIS TO DENY THIS MOTION. ORDER IS SIGNED. (SEE SCANNED ORDER FOR DETAILS) JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, CLERK: M. DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-03-2019 Sustained
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT JUSTIN CHEUNG, KELVIN CHEUNG, VILMA CHEUNG's DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF'S 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT.

Defendants Justin, Kelvin, and Vilma Cheung's demurrer to the second amended complaint is sustained in part and overruled in part.

The demurrers to the conversion causes of action (1-3) are overruled. Whether plaintiff can present clear and convincing evidence that Stanley intended for the funds and other property to go to plaintiff and whether the funds are sufficiently identifiable are factual issues that cannot be resolved on demurrer. The face of the complaint does not reveal that the claims are time-barred. A demurrer can be used only to challenge defects that appear on the face of the complaint or matters outside the pleading that are judicially noticeable; the court does not consider discovery responses or other extrinsic evidence. Both parties' requests for judicial notice of discovery responses and related motions are denied.

The demurrer to the accounting cause of action is overruled. "A cause of action for an accounting requires a showing that a relationship exists between the plaintiff and defendant that requires an accounting, and that some balance is due the plaintiff that can only be ascertained by an accounting." (Jolley v. Chase Home Finance, LLC (2013) 213 Cal.App.4th 872, 910.) As the administrator of Stanley's estate, plaintiff sufficiently alleges a relationship between the parties to support the claim.

The demurrer to the UCL cause of action is sustained with leave to amend. If plaintiff seeks to allege an unlawful business practice, she needs to identify the statutes that were breached. (See Khoury v. Maly's of California, Inc. (1993) 14 Cal.App.4th 612, 619 ["A plaintiff alleging unfair business practices under these statutes must state with reasonable particularity the facts supporting the statutory elements of the violation."].) The demurrer to the declaratory relief cause of action is overruled.

Lastly, plaintiff has leave to plead the civil conspiracy, unjust enrichment, and injunctive relief allegations as remedies and/or general charging allegations. They do not constitute separate causes of action.

Defendants' remaining arguments lack merit. The financial institutions and insurance carriers are not indispensable parties, nor is the second amended complaint fatally uncertain. No breach of contract is alleged.

Order is signed.***(see scanned order for details)
